sections.title: Proper vs Improper Subsets
points: A proper subset is smaller than the original set.
A set cannot be a proper subset of itself.
If A = {a, b}, then {a} is proper but {a, b} is not.

sections.title: The Shortcut Formula
points: Total subsets of a set with n members is 2ⁿ.
Proper subsets are one less than total subsets.

sections.title: Why We Subtract 1
points: The one subset we remove is the set itself.
This subset is called the improper subset.
Every set always contains itself as a subset.

cards.fact: A subset that is strictly smaller than the original set.
cards.mnemonic: Proper means it is not the full set.

cards.fact: A subset that is the set itself.
cards.mnemonic: Improper is the whole set, so it is not proper.
